pattern: An alternating array is fully described by just two repeating values, one for every even slot and one for every odd slot, so the fewest edits is a question of how much of each parity group you can leave untouched by picking its most common value.
hints: Split the array into the values sitting at even positions and the values sitting at odd positions, and tally how often each value shows up in each group.
If the most frequent even-position value and the most frequent odd-position value are different from each other, keep both and change everything else.

If those two most-frequent values happen to be the same number, you cannot keep both groups at their favorite value, so compare keeping the even group's favorite with the odd group's second favorite against the odd group's favorite with the even group's second favorite.

edge_inputs.description: single element is trivially alternating already
two equal elements need one change
two distinct elements are already alternating
every element is the same value, forcing the tie-break rule
the top even and odd values coincide but second choices differ in count
only the even-position group has a repeat
odd length array leaves an unmatched final even slot
both groups already share no common favorite despite duplicates

stress.description: random arrays with a small value alphabet to force frequent ties
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 5) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]

stress.description: random arrays across the full value range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 100000) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
